title: Leandra australis
taxonomy: Leandra australis is a species of flowering plant in the family Melastomataceae. It belongs to the genus Leandra, which is a group of plants commonly known as the melastome family. The family Melastomataceae is a part of the order Myrtales, which is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Onagraceae and Penaeaceae. The order Myrtales is within the clade Rosids, which is a large group of flowering plants that also includes the families Fabaceae and Malvaceae. Leandra australis is native to South America, specifically in the tropical regions of Brazil and other countries.
uses: Leandra australis is a plant species native to South America, and research suggests it has been traditionally used in folk medicine for various purposes. Leandra australis has been studied for its potential anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which could contribute to its medicinal uses.
